data.title: Yara International reports Q2 results
data.description:
• Yara International press release (OTCPK:YARIY): Q2 GAAP EPS of $1.62.
  • Revenue of $3.95B (+11.9% Y/Y).

data.title: Husqvarna AB reports Q2 results
data.description:
• Husqvarna AB press release (OTCPK:HUSQF): Q2 GAAP EPS of SEK2.76.
  • Revenue of SEK15.28B (-1.0% Y/Y).
  • Operating income was SEK 2,063M (vs. 1,899M prior) and the operating margin was 13.5% (vs. 12.3%).

data.title: Trump threatens to sue WSJ over Epstein story, clears DOJ to release grand jury testimony
data.description:
President Donald Trump has authorized the Justice Department to seek the release of grand jury testimony related to the Jeffrey Epstein case, following rising pressure from his supporters for transparency.

The move comes just hours after The Wall Street Journal published a story alleging Trump once sent Epstein a suggestive birthday letter—an accusation Trump fiercely denies.

Calling the letter “FAKE,” Trump blasted the WSJ, its parent company News Corp. (NASDAQ:NWS) and owner Rupert Murdoch, in a Thursday night Truth Social post, threatening legal action and accusing them of running a “false, malicious, and defamatory” article.

“I told Rupert Murdoch it was a Scam, that he shouldn’t print this Fake Story. But he did, and now I’m going to sue his ass off, and that of his third rate newspaper,” Trump wrote on Truth Social.

He claimed both he and press secretary Karoline Leavitt informed WSJ editor-in-chief Emma Tucker the document was fabricated, but she proceeded with the story regardless.

The WSJ report claims the letter was part of a leather-bound birthday album compiled by Ghislaine Maxwell in 2003 for Epstein. According to the report, Justice Department officials reviewed the album years ago, though it's unclear if the review occurred during Trump’s presidency.

The Trump-era DOJ concluded there was no evidence Epstein was murdered or maintained a “client list.” Maxwell is currently serving a 20-year prison sentence for sex trafficking and related crimes.

Dear Readers: We recognize that politics often intersect with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: UK-based Reckitt to sell Essential Home division in a $4.8B deal, including debt
data.description:
• UK-based consumer firm Reckitt Benckiser (OTCPK:RBGPF) (OTCPK:RBGLY) will sell its Essential Home business to private equity firm Advent International in a deal valued at up to $4.8 billion including debt.
  • This transaction is part of Reckitt’s ongoing strategy to refocus on its core, higher-growth health and hygiene brands.
  • Reckitt will retain a 30% equity stake in Essential Home after the sale.
  • Essential Home division contributed roughly 13% to group revenue in Q1 and saw a sales drop of 7%.
  • The completion of the transaction is expected by this year end, pending regulatory approvals and completion of separation activities.

data.title: WaFd GAAP EPS of $0.73 beats by $0.04, revenue of $186.26M misses by $2.57M
data.description:
• WaFd press release (NASDAQ:WAFD): Q3 GAAP EPS of $0.73 beats by $0.04.
  • Revenue of $186.26M (-4.2% Y/Y) misses by $2.57M.
  • Net interest margin at 2.69% for the quarter compared to 2.55% for Q2.
  • Delinquencies down $4 million to 0.26% of total loans compared to 0.27% for Q2.
  • Efficiency ratio decreased to 56.01% Q3 compared to 58.31% in the prior quarter.
  • Customer deposits totaled $21.4 billion as of June 30, 2025, largely unchanged from September 30, 2024.
  • The company recorded a $2.0 million provision for credit losses in the third fiscal quarter of 2025 compared to a provision of $2.8 million the prior quarter.

data.title: BancFirst GAAP EPS of $1.85 beats by $0.12, revenue of $169.31M misses by $0.99M
data.description:
• BancFirst press release (NASDAQ:BANF): Q2 GAAP EPS of $1.85 beats by $0.12.
  • Revenue of $169.31M (+10.1% Y/Y) misses by $0.99M.
  • Net interest margin was virtually unchanged at 3.75% for the second quarter of 2025 and 3.76% for the second quarter of 2024.
  • The company recorded a provision for credit losses on loans of $1.2 million in the second quarter of 2025 compared to $3.4 million for the same period in 2024.
  • Deposits totaled $12.1 billion, an increase of $337.6 million from year-end 2024.
  • Sweep accounts totaled $5.3 billion at June 30, 2025, up $66.8 million from December 31, 2024.

data.title: First Financial Bankshares GAAP EPS of $0.47 beats by $0.01, revenue of $156.6M misses by $2.76M
data.description:
• First Financial Bankshares press release (NASDAQ:FFIN): Q2 GAAP EPS of $0.47 beats by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $156.6M (+16.4% Y/Y) misses by $2.76M.
  • The net interest margin, on a taxable equivalent basis, was 3.81 percent for the second quarter of 2025 compared to 3.48 percent for the second quarter of 2024 and 3.74 percent in the first quarter of 2025. 
  • The company recorded a provision for credit losses of $3.13 million for the second quarter of 2025 compared to a provision for credit losses of $5.89 million for the second quarter of 2024 and $3.53 million for the first quarter 2025.
  • At June 30, 2025, the allowance for credit losses totaled $102.79 million, or 1.27 percent of loans held-for-investment, compared to $95.17 million, or 1.27 percent of loans, at June 30, 2024.
  • The company's efficiency ratio was 44.97 percent for the second quarter of 2025 compared to 47.41 percent for the second quarter of 2024 largely due to the increase in net interest income.

data.title: Independent Bank Non-GAAP EPS of $1.25 misses by $0.18, revenue of $181.8M misses by $61.1M
data.description:
• Independent Bank press release (NASDAQ:INDB): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.25 misses by $0.18.
  • Revenue of $181.8M (+6.8% Y/Y) misses by $61.1M.
  • "In consideration of the company’s current strong capital position, the company is announcing a new stock repurchase plan, which authorizes repurchases by the company of up to $150 million in common stock and is scheduled to expire on July 16, 2026."
  • On an operating basis, the company generated a return on average assets and a return on average common equity of 1.09% and 6.99%, respectively, for the second quarter of 2025, as compared to 0.94% and 6.05%, respectively, for the prior quarter.
  • The company’s net interest margin of 3.37% decreased 5 basis points compared to the prior quarter, while the core margin was unchanged from the prior quarter at 3.37%.
  • Deposit balances of $15.9 billion at June 30, 2025 increased $217.7 million, or 1.4% (5.6% annualized), from the first quarter of 2025.
  • Loan balances of $14.5 billion at June 30, 2025 increased $41.9 million, or 0.3% (1.2% annualized), from the first quarter of 2025.
  • Tangible book value of $48.80 per share at June 30, 2025 grew by $0.99 from the prior quarter.
  • The company’s tax rate of 22.35% for the second quarter of 2025 remained consistent with the prior quarter.

data.title: BlackSky Technology prices $160M senior notes
data.description:
• BlackSky Technology (NYSE:BKSY) priced $160M principal amount of 8.25% convertible senior notes due 2033 in a private offering to persons reasonably believed to be qualified institutional buyers.
  • The offering size was increased from the previously announced offering size of $125M notes.
  • Underwriters have an option to purchase an additional $25M principal amount of the notes.
  • The sale of the notes is expected to close on July 22, 2025.
  • BlackSky estimates that the net proceeds will be approximately $153.7M.
  • The company expects to use net proceeds for general corporate purposes.

data.title: Trump grants two-year regulatory relief to coal, chemical, and high-pollution industries
data.description:
President Donald Trump on Thursday granted two years of regulatory relief from certain regulations for coal-fired power plants, chemical manufacturers, and other high-pollution industries. 

The move is aimed at rolling back environmental rules put in place during the Biden administration, which Trump argues are overly restrictive.

In a series of proclamations issued late Thursday, Trump granted temporary exemptions to several industries he described as critical to national security.

The proclamations cover coal-fired power plants, taconite iron ore processing facilities used to make steel, and chemical manufacturers that help produce semiconductors and medical device sterilizers.

The proclamations allow the facilities to comply with Environmental Protection Agency standards that were in place before rules imposed in recent years by President Joe Biden's administration, the White House said.

"Biden-era emissions standards impose costly and, in some cases, unattainable compliance requirements on these industries essential to national interests," the White House said in a fact sheet.
